21.correct: The five Sikhs initiated by Guru Gobind Singh when he founded the Khalsa in 1699 are known as the 'Panj Pyare' (the Five Beloved Ones).

wrong.B: 'Panj Piara' is just a misspelling, not the recognised term.
wrong.C: 'Panj Sikh' is not the historical name.

21.fact: The Khalsa was founded at Anandpur Sahib on Baisakhi in 1699.

22.correct: In free fall (ignoring air resistance), just before hitting the ground the object's potential energy is at a minimum and its kinetic energy is at a maximum, as PE converts into KE.

wrong.A: PE is minimum (not maximum) at the lowest point.
wrong.C: Both energies are not zero; KE is maximum.
wrong.D: In ideal free fall total mechanical energy is conserved, so no energy is lost.

22.fact: This illustrates conservation of mechanical energy: PE + KE remains constant throughout the fall.

23.correct: 'Interpreter of Maladies', a collection of short stories that won the Pulitzer Prize (2000), was written by Jhumpa Lahiri.

wrong.A: Arundhati Roy wrote 'The God of Small Things'.
wrong.C: Kiran Desai wrote 'The Inheritance of Loss'.
wrong.D: Anita Desai wrote works like 'Clear Light of Day'.

23.fact: Jhumpa Lahiri's stories often explore the Indian immigrant experience in the United States.

24.correct: The Ganga-Brahmaputra river system forms the world's largest delta - the Sundarbans delta - before flowing into the Bay of Bengal.

wrong.A: The Indus forms a delta in Pakistan, far smaller than the Ganga-Brahmaputra delta.
wrong.C: The Godavari delta is on the east coast but much smaller.
wrong.D: The Krishna delta is also smaller.

24.fact: The delta spans India (West Bengal) and Bangladesh and hosts the Sundarbans mangroves.

25.correct: The main force that keeps a satellite in orbit (period 90 minutes) is the Earth's gravitational force, which provides the necessary centripetal force.

wrong.B: The 'centrifugal force' is a pseudo-force in the rotating frame, not the real force holding the satellite.
wrong.C: Earth's magnetic force is far too weak to hold a satellite in orbit.
wrong.D: Electromagnetic force is not what keeps a satellite orbiting.

25.fact: In orbit, gravity continuously pulls the satellite towards Earth, bending its path into a closed orbit.

26.correct: If the Moon completes its orbit (360 degrees) in about 27 days, it moves roughly 360/27 = 13.3 degrees per day.

wrong.B: 27 degrees is the number of days, not the daily angular motion.
wrong.C: 360 degrees is the full orbit, covered over the whole 27 days.
wrong.D: 7 degrees per day would imply an orbit much slower than 27 days.

26.fact: This daily shift is why the Moon rises roughly 50 minutes later each day.

27.correct: The INSAT-3D meteorological satellite helped monitor cyclones such as Hudhud and Phailin, providing weather imaging and early warnings.

wrong.B: Megha-Tropiques studies the tropical water cycle but is not the cyclone-tracking satellite referenced.
wrong.C: GSAT-7 is a naval communication satellite.
wrong.D: RISAT-2 is a radar imaging satellite used mainly for surveillance.

27.fact: INSAT-3D carries an imager and a sounder used by the India Meteorological Department for forecasting.

28.correct: The Second Five-Year Plan (1956-61), based on the Mahalanobis model, focused mainly on industrialization and the building of heavy industries.

wrong.A: Promoting handicraft exports was not the Second Plan's main focus.
wrong.C: Expanding the service sector was not the priority of the Second Plan.
wrong.D: It did not aim at full privatization of agriculture.

28.fact: The Second Plan set up public-sector steel plants at Bhilai, Rourkela and Durgapur.

29.correct: Neeraj Chopra won India's only athletics medal at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics - a gold in the javelin throw.

wrong.B: This option does not name the medallist.
wrong.C: Avinash Sable competed in the steeplechase but did not win an athletics medal at Tokyo.
wrong.D: Hima Das did not win a medal at the Tokyo Olympics.

29.fact: Neeraj Chopra's gold was India's first-ever Olympic medal in athletics.

30.correct: The World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) oversees the global fight against doping in sport.

wrong.B: The IOC organises the Olympics; it works with WADA but does not run global anti-doping.
wrong.C: USADA is the United States' national anti-doping agency.
wrong.D: NADA is India's National Anti-Doping Agency.

30.fact: WADA was established in 1999 and maintains the World Anti-Doping Code and the prohibited-substances list.

31.correct: India's trade policy from the 1950s to the 1980s emphasised import substitution and protectionism - encouraging domestic production behind high tariffs and import controls.

wrong.A: Full liberalization came only with the 1991 reforms.
wrong.C: Export promotion through free trade was not the dominant pre-1991 strategy.
wrong.D: Foreign portfolio investment was not emphasised in that era.

31.fact: Import substitution aimed at self-reliance but is criticised for creating inefficient, sheltered industries.

32.correct: The Food Corporation of India (FCI) was established in 1965 to procure, store and distribute food grains and ensure food security.
32.fact: The FCI manages the buffer stock and supports the Minimum Support Price and Public Distribution System.

33.correct: The Atal Pension Yojana (APY) covers citizens in the 18 to 40 years age group, who then receive a guaranteed pension from age 60.

wrong.B: The upper entry age is 40, not 60.
wrong.C: The entry age starts at 18, not 21.
wrong.D: Neither the lower nor upper limits in this option are correct.

33.fact: APY is aimed mainly at workers in the unorganised sector and gives a guaranteed monthly pension after 60.

34.correct: The Western Ghats are also known as the Sahyadri Hills, running parallel to India's western coast.

wrong.B: The Vindhya Range is a separate central-Indian range.
wrong.C: The Aravalli Hills are in the north-west (Rajasthan).
wrong.D: The Nilgiri Hills are only a part of the Western Ghats, not the whole range's name.

34.fact: The Western Ghats are a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a major biodiversity hotspot.

35.correct: The economic liberalization policy launched in 1991 opened up trade and reduced controls, with the aim (among others) of boosting India's export growth.

wrong.A: Import substitution was the older, pre-1991 strategy being abandoned.
wrong.B: The Exim policy framework existed earlier; the 1991 landmark was broad liberalization.
wrong.D: The Green Revolution was an agricultural strategy, not a trade-policy reform.

35.fact: The 1991 reforms slashed tariffs, dismantled licensing and devalued the rupee to spur exports.

36.correct: During the Sangam Age the Tamil region was dominated by three important ruling chiefdoms - the Cholas, the Cheras and the Pandyas.

wrong.A: Chalukyas and Mauryas were not Sangam-age Tamil chiefdoms.
wrong.C: Satavahanas and Guptas were northern/Deccan powers.
wrong.D: Pallavas, Mauryas and Nandas were not the Sangam-age Tamil trio.

36.fact: The emblems of the three dynasties were the tiger (Chola), the bow (Chera) and the fish (Pandya).

37.correct: The Kashi Mahajanapada corresponds to the present-day city of Banaras (Varanasi).

wrong.A: Jaipur is a much later Rajput city.
wrong.B: Mathura was a separate centre (Surasena Mahajanapada).
wrong.D: Allahabad (Prayagraj) was not the Kashi Mahajanapada.

37.fact: Kashi was one of the sixteen Mahajanapadas (great kingdoms) of ancient India.

38.correct: Swami Vivekananda's guru was Ramakrishna Paramhansa, the saint of Dakshineswar.

wrong.A: Ramananda was a medieval Bhakti saint, not Vivekananda's guru.
wrong.B: Adi Shankaracharya lived in the 8th century.
wrong.C: Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was a 15th-16th century Vaishnava saint.

38.fact: Vivekananda founded the Ramakrishna Mission in 1897 in memory of his guru.

39.correct: Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan ('Frontier Gandhi') was strongly opposed to the Partition of India and worked for Hindu-Muslim unity.

wrong.A: Muhammad Ali Jinnah led the demand for Pakistan.
wrong.B: Chaudhry Rahmat Ali coined the name 'Pakistan'.
wrong.C: Liaquat Ali Khan was a Muslim League leader who became Pakistan's first PM.

39.fact: Ghaffar Khan founded the non-violent 'Khudai Khidmatgar' (Red Shirts) movement among the Pashtuns.

40.correct: In 2025 the IMF projected India's GDP growth for 2026 at about 6.4%, indicating continued strong expansion.

wrong.B: 7.1% is higher than the projected figure.

40.fact: India has remained the fastest-growing major economy in recent IMF projections.
